SCOAS Execution Report - bitbucket

Timestamp: 2026-01-29-11-30

Summary

1427
Rule Violations
4.47
Average Violations/Route
0
Routes Without Violations
319
Routes Analyzed
858
Responses Analyzed
If your OpenAPI Specification file contains status code misuses, they should be fixed based on the rule descriptions that are provided. The rule sources provide references to the standards (RFCs) that were used to define the rules.

Details

  • 312 violations of the rule has-422-if-params
  • 300 violations of the rule has-406-if-accept
  • 267 violations of the rule has-400-if-params
  • 95 violations of the rule has-413-if-content-length
  • 95 violations of the rule has-415-if-content-type
  • 95 violations of the rule has-422-if-payload
  • 79 violations of the rule no-403-if-no-401
  • 66 violations of the rule has-404-if-path
  • 60 violations of the rule has-400-if-payload
  • 31 violations of the rule has-204-if-no-content
  • 12 violations of the rule has-200-if-get
  • 6 violations of the rule no-non-standard-codes
  • 5 violations of the rule no-204-if-content
  • 3 violations of the rule has-200-or-201-or-204-if-post
  • 1 violations of the rule has-200-or-204-if-delete

Violation #1

Route: DELETE /addon

Responses: 204 No Content, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden

Parameters: /

Rule ID: has-406-if-accept

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 406 Not Acceptable in case the sever does not support the Accept header specified in the request. Only applies to routes that respond with content.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Responses: 201 Created, 404 Not Found, 409 Conflict

Parameters: workspace (path), repo_slug (path)

Rule ID: has-422-if-payload

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 422 Unprocessable Content if the method contains a payload (in case of invalid semantics).

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.21 (422 Unprocessable Content)

Responses: default (OpenAPI)

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.4 (403 Forbidden) | RFC 9110, Section 15.5.2 (401 Unauthorized)

Violation #1292

Route: GET /workspaces/{workspace}/pipelines-config/identity/oidc/.well-known/openid-configuration

Responses: 200 OK, 404 Not Found

Parameters: workspace (path)

Rule ID: has-204-if-no-content

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 204 No Content if a response in the 2xx Successful range does not have content.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.3.5 (204 No Content) | RFC 9110, Section 15.3 (Successful 2xx)

Responses: 200 OK, 404 Not Found

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Violation #1411

Route: GET /workspaces/{workspace}/projects/{project_key}/permissions-config/users

Responses: 200 OK, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found

Parameters: project_key (path), workspace (path)

Rule ID: has-422-if-params

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 422 Unprocessable Content if the method contains parameters (in case of invalid semantics).

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.21 (422 Unprocessable Content)

Violation #1412

Route: DELETE /workspaces/{workspace}/projects/{project_key}/permissions-config/users/{selected_user_id}

Responses: 204 No Content, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found

Parameters: project_key (path), selected_user_id (path), workspace (path)

Rule ID: has-400-if-params

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 400 Bad Request if the method contains parameters (in case of invalid syntax).

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.1 (400 Bad Request)

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 413 Content Too Large in case the server does not support the Content-Length header specified in the request.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.14 (413 Content Too Large) | RFC 9110, Section 8.6 (Content-Length)
